key_insight: The paper proposes an automated pipeline that turns symbolic reasoning algorithms into solvable numerical recurrences, combining human-provided insights with abstract interpretation and recurrence-solving techniques.
engineering_relevance: This could make complexity analysis of advanced automated reasoners faster, more scalable, and less dependent on manual proof effort.
method: User supplies key metrics and helper lemmas for the target algorithm.
Higher-order abstract interpretation extracts generalized recurrence equations from symbolic programs.
A solver uses CAS, SMT, and pre/postfixpoint methods to infer and verify complexity bounds.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theory and tool under development, with examples drawn from super-exponential quantifier elimination procedures, but does not present experimental results.
watch_item: The approach still depends on expert-provided metrics and lemmas, so full automation and robustness will depend on how well these inputs can be supplied and generalized.
paper_title: Towards an Automated Reasoning Tool for Complexity Analysis of Automated Reasoners
